reasoning: Attacked and held. (1) TRENCH vertices ARE `.WP WPN` byte-for-byte. `.WP WPN` (WSOBJ.MAC:560, `.S=8`) decodes via the `.PH` macro (radix-16, `.1'*.S`, no decimal suffix): `.PH -20,0,-18` = [-0x20*8, 0, -0x18*8] = [-256,0,-192]; the 8 rows give outer rect X=+/-256 Z=+/-192 (pts 0-3) and inner rect X=+/-128 Z=+/-64 (pts 4-7), all Y=0 — identical to models.ts:548-557. (2) `.WGD WPN` (WSOBJ.MAC:1803-1813) strokes ONLY the two rectangles: `PLOT 0 / DRAWTO 1,2,3,0` = edges [0,1][1,2][2,3][3,0]; `BDRAWTO 4,5,6,7,4` = blank-move to 4 then [4,5][5,6][6,7][7,4]. I decoded the DRAWTO/BDRAWTO macros (WSOBJ.MAC:1653-1693): the B-prefix vertex is a pen-up (blank) move, the rest are bright draws, indices are 0-based decimal. So the governing draw list contains NO connecting rails. Ours (models.ts:558-565) reproduces both rectangles set-equal but ADDS four fabricated rails [0,4][1,5][2,6][3,7] — the model's own comment admits 'story 8-5 adds the CATWALK RAILS.' Those 4 edges are pure fabrication vs `.WGD WPN`. (3) TRENCH_SQUARE (models.ts:675-679) is a hand-authored flat +/-40 square (its comment: PROVISIONAL, 'no recovered exact ROM shape data'); the ROM object it stands in for — the shootable 'trench green square' / decorative panel scored TSCPNL=50 — is exactly WPN ('WALL PANEL'), so it is a second, geometrically-wrong derivation of the same one WPN object. Verdict holds unchanged: drop the 4 rails to match `.WGD WPN` (size s); reconcile TRENCH_SQUARE onto WPN.

Attacked and held. `.WGD PORT` (WSOBJ.MAC:1855-1876) switches pen exactly three times: `MOVD #VGCGRN*100+VGCOPC ;OUTER BASE` (DRAWTO 9,8,4 / BDRAWTO 6,10,11,7), `MOVD #VGCTRQ*100+VGCOPC ;INNER BERM` (DRAWTO 6,2 / three BDRAWTO runs), and `MOVD #VGCRED*100+VGCOPC!0FF ;PORTHOLE` (DRAWTO 2,0,1,3). Color equates (WSGLOB.MAC:57-59): VGCGRN==2 (green), VGCTRQ==3 (turquoise), VGCRED==4 (red). The porthole (the +/-96 inner square, pts 0-3) is explicitly stroked RED. render.ts (line 200/372) draws the whole plate in ONE drawWireframe pass with PORT_GLOW = GLOW_FOR['Exhaust Port'] = '#ff9f0a' (wireframe.ts:34, 'target amber') — a single amber hue that matches none of the ROM's three pens, and in particular renders the porthole amber instead of red. The models.ts comment already concedes this is a one-model/one-colour simplification ('no AC asks for the colours'). Correctly classified wont_fix DIVERGENCE; numbers verified, no corrections.

Attacked and held. TSCFRC (WSGAS.MAC:509-513) is a 5-row BCD table: 00,50,00=5,000 / 01,00,00=10,000 / 02,50,00=25,000 / 05,00,00=50,000 / 10,00,00=100,000. GETFRP (WSGAS.MAC:404-416) indexes GM.WAV*3 into TSCFRC and clamps at TSCFRZ-3 for GM.WAV>=5 (CMPB #TSCFRZ-TSCFRC/3 -> #5, left-to-right MACRO-11 => (15/3)=5). So GM.WAV 0->5k, 1->10k, 2->25k, 3->50k, 4->100k (direct index), >=5->100k (clamp) — the finding's '0=5k,1=10k,2=25k,3=50k,4+=100k' is exact (the ROM comment 'LEVEL 5 AND ABOVE' is 1-based human levels; GM.WAV=4 already lands on 100k). SCRFRC (WSGAS.MAC:440-442) is invoked once per clean trench from WSBASE.MAC:1169 ('SCORE ONE FOR ASSERTIVENESS TRAINING(FORCEFUL)'). Ours: sim.ts:751 `TRENCH_BONUS + (clean ? FORCE_BONUS : 0)` with FORCE_BONUS = 5000 flat (state.ts:482) — a single constant that cannot express the table; waves 2+ (GM.WAV) are 2x-20x low. The proposed fix table [5000,10000,25000,50000,100000] clamped is correct; the port must map our 1-based `wave` to GM.WAV via wave-1 before indexing (our wave 1 == GM.WAV 0 == 5000, which is why the flat value happens to match only the first trench).
corrections: Minor (non-blocking): the proposed FORCE_BONUS_BY_WAVE is at GM.WAV (0-based) granularity, so a faithful port indexes it by (wave-1) since the clone's `wave` is 1-based; clamp any index >=4 to the 100,000 row.

reasoning: Attacked with an exhaustive hunt per the brief; the BOOK_WAS_WRONG holds. (1) No BCD-disguised threshold: grep of every shipped .MAC/.DOC for 40000/80000/400000/800000/a40000/a80000 returns ZERO hits. (2) No score-threshold logic can exist because the score bytes are never compared: grep for any CMP/SUB against S.SC21/S.SC43/S.SC65/S.SC87 across all WS*.MAC returns nothing (exit 1) — the BCD score is only ever ADDED to (ADUSCR, WSGAS:484-503) and displayed, never read for a threshold. (3) Every STA/DEC of S.GAS in shipped code is accounted for and none is score-gated: init (WSMAIN:1297, 6-9 per operator option), the per-hit DEC + underflow pin-to-dead (WSGAS:76,79), and ADCGAS's add+clamp (WSGAS:46-53). (4) ADCGAS is the ONLY replenish path and is called ONLY from WSMAIN:1975, immediately gated by 'LDA PT.LIV ;DID PLAYER KILL DETH STAR? / IFNE' (WSMAIN:1973-1974) — a Death-Star-kill bonus of 0-3 units (OPTS1 high nibble, `ANDA #03`), clamped to the STARTING amount (OPTS1+1 low nibble +6 = 6-9). (5) POSITIVE CORROBORATION: the official option sheet SWOPTS.DOC (17-MAY-83) lists exactly two shield options — 'STARTING SHIELDS' 6/7/8/9 and 'BONUS SHIELDS FOR DESTROYING DEATH STAR' 0/1/2/3 with the note 'Shield strength will not go higher than STARTING SHIELDS level' — and NO score-threshold bonus. The disasm-derived secondary doc's 'extra-life text a40000/a80000' (docs/star-wars-1983-source-findings.md:458) drives no shield/life mechanism in the primary source; ours (state.ts:135 EXTRA_LIFE_THRESHOLDS=[400_000,800_000], applied uncapped in finalizeScore sim.ts:372-374) is invented and can push lives past the ROM's 6-9 max. Fix stands: drop the invented thresholds; refill via an ADCGAS-style bonus on Death-Star-kill, clamped to starting shields.
corrections: The finding's S.GAS-write enumeration omits WSGAS:79 (STA S.GAS 'SET TO DEAD', the underflow pin to -1) — it belongs to the per-hit DEC/death path, not a replenish, so it does not weaken the thesis. The ADCGAS clamp is specifically to STARTING SHIELDS (6-9, operator-set), matching SWOPTS.DOC 'will not go higher than STARTING SHIELDS level' — worth carrying into the fix so the cap is the option value, not a hard-coded 9.

reasoning: Attacked and held. The ROM caps shield loss at one per hit-animation cycle via two gates. BG1GLW (WSGLOW.MAC:58-64) re-arms the shield glow ONLY when GS.GLW==0 ('?READY FOR ANOTHER HIT?'), then sets S.GLW=10 and INC GS.GLW (busy). DO1GAS (WSGAS.MAC:63-104) enters the decrement block only when GS.GLW>0 AND GS.HIT<=0 ('?AND DONE WITH PREVIOUS GAS CHANGE?', line 68); it then sets GS.HIT=1 and does a single DEC S.GAS. Both GS.HIT and GS.GLW are cleared only at the END of the multi-frame gauge-redraw animation (GSVNEW, WSGAS.MAC:258-260, 'AND TELL REST OF WORLD WE ARE READY FOR MORE'). So while the animation runs, BG1GLW cannot re-arm and DO1GAS cannot re-decrement — one shield per event. BG1GLW is the universal 'took a hit' entry (fireball WSGUNS:1152, tower/bunker crash WSGRND:911/951, trench wall WSPANL:214/382, cockpit WSMAIN:1908 whose comment reads 'BASHED OUR NOSE -- LIMITS REPEATS' — the ROM naming the lockout outright). Ours (sim.ts:307-323) accumulates `damage++` independently across the enemy-collision filter AND the fireball-collision filter with NO cooldown/i-frame/glow state (grep of src/core confirms only fire cooldowns exist, no damage window), then `lives = max(0, lives - damage)`. So a simultaneous cluster strips multiple shields in one frame and a rapid sequence drains with no invulnerability. DIVERGENCE holds; fix needs a hit-cooldown flag on GameState.
corrections: Precision note (does not change verdict): the re-hit lockout is governed by GS.GLW/GS.HIT being cleared at the completion of the gauge-redraw animation (GSVNEW), not directly by the S.GLW=10 glow-display counter the finding cites; the ~10-game-frame / ~0.49s-at-20.508Hz figure is a good order-of-magnitude estimate of that window, not an exact ROM-pinned duration.

reasoning: Attacked per the coverage-review flag; it is HONEST and holds, with a scoping correction. Arithmetic is exact: GD$MDT ==1C00-200/2+200 evaluates left-to-right (MACRO-11, no precedence) as ((0x1C00-0x200)/2)+0x200 = (0x1A00/2)+0x200 = 0x0D00+0x200 = 0x0F00 = 3840, which is also the midpoint of the vertical travel band [GD$MNT=0x200=512, GD$MXT=0x1C00=7168] (WSMAIN.MAC:2597-2599). SKIM_ALTITUDE=128 (state.ts:339) = 3840/30, and 30 is GROUND_MODEL_SCALE (render.ts:157, =1/30). The equivalence is NOT a numerical coincidence dressed up as fidelity: render.ts:172-177 DERIVES the tower-seating lift as GD$MDT * GROUND_MODEL_SCALE = 128 directly from the ROM constant and seats every ground object's model-z=0 there — i.e. the ship at altitude 128 flies exactly at the height where the ROM recentres ground objects ('OFFSET HITE TO MID OF PLAYERS HITE'). TOWER_HEIGHT is scaled by the same 1/30 (10560 raw -> 352), so the scale-free invariant SKIM_ALTITUDE:TOWER_HEIGHT = GD$MDT:tower-top ~= 3840/10560 ~= 0.36 is genuinely authentic. The finding discloses the 1/30 shell factor in both its title and reasoning, so it does not overclaim byte-exactness. It survives as CONFIRMED but should be re-characterized, not left implying a scale-free byte match like D-001..D-012.
corrections: Re-class as 'CONFIRMED within the 1/30 ground-render convention.' The 1/30 (GROUND_MODEL_SCALE) is a SHELL render choice, not a ROM constant, so unlike the byte-exact maze confirms this equivalence is not scale-free at the absolute level (128 != 3840 without it). State the load-bearing, scale-free fidelity explicitly: SKIM_ALTITUDE = GD$MDT x GROUND_MODEL_SCALE seats towers at the ship's skim height, and the altitude:tower-height ratio (~0.36) mirrors the ROM's GD$MDT:tower-top — that ratio, not the absolute 128<->3840 mapping, is the authentic quantity. Also note the surface is anisotropically scaled: maze X/Y/depth are raw ROM units 1:1 (D-001..D-012) while the vertical band is 1/30.
